summary: Merged fighter-only 100% decompilation progress split from PR #2345. The PR replaced six asm stubs with matching C across common fighter death/respawn logic, catch-pull animation handling, and Kirby copy-hat setup/matrix display code. The changes are mostly match-focused and preserve known layout quirks through explicit padding, temporary variables, casts, and a dont_inline pragma.
what_changed: Implemented `fn_800D54A4` in `ft_0D31.c`, updating a fighter accessory joint translation from current position, facing direction, and possible partnered fighter Y-position adjustment before calling `HSD_JObjSetTranslate`.

Implemented `ftCo_Rebirth_Phys` in `ft_0D31.c`, computing respawn-platform movement velocity from stage/player spawn offsets when not already respawned, or copying velocity from another player entity when `x221F_b4` is set.

Implemented `ftCo_800D41C4` in `ft_0D31.c`, setting up the `ftCo_MS_DeadUpStarIce` / star-KO-like dead-up sequence, including flags, counters, motion-state change, SFX, player notification, and common fighter state calls.
Implemented `ftCo_CatchPull_Anim` in `ftCo_Attack100.c`, handling Link/Young Link hookshot, Samus grapple, and default catch-pull transition conditions before calling `fn_800DA1D8`.

Implemented `ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0` in `ftKb_Init.c`, copying Kirby hat matrix data from head bone part 6, setting JObj matrix-independence/user-matrix flags, dirtying the matrix, and dispatching `HSD_JObjDispAll`.

Implemented `ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4` in `ftKb_Init.c` for Pichu-copy Kirby hat setup, including hat allocation, parts/visibility initialization, animation setup, dynamics table linkage, color overlay copy, and model scale update.
Added `#include <baselib/jobj.h>` to support the Kirby JObj matrix code.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scope narrow: the body explicitly says it was split from #2345 and contains fighter-related 100% matches only.
Used targeted `PAD_STACK` calls in several functions to preserve stack layout for matching without pretending the padding has semantic meaning.
Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around `ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4`, documenting and containing an inlining-sensitive match requirement.

Preserved decompiler/matching artifacts where needed, such as `Fighter* fp = fp = gobj->user_data;`, local temporaries like `new_var`, explicit casts through `u8*`, and typed reinterpretations of struct fields.

Handled per-character catch-pull behavior explicitly by switching on `fp->kind`, using Link/Young Link hookshot storage separately from Samus grapple storage, and falling back to frame/throw-flag logic for other cases.
In Kirby hat matrix update, guarded early on missing hat JObj and disabled `x2225_b2` before touching matrix/display state, keeping the C control flow simple and match-friendly.
decomp_lessons: For 100% matches, preserve non-obvious local-variable shape, stack padding, and cast style even when equivalent cleaner C exists.

Kirby copy-hat initialization relies on multiple subsystems in sequence: hat descriptor lookup, HSD object allocation, parts setup, animation table setup, dynamics/visibility table wiring, color copy, and model-scale refresh. Future hat-related matches should compare this ordering carefully.

JObj matrix callbacks may need both data copy calls and flag mutations: `HSD_JObjCopyMtx`, `JOBJ_USER_DEF_MTX`, `JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_PARENT`, `JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT`, `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty`, then display dispatch.
Catch-pull animation logic is item-state-driven for tether characters: NULL item pointers and item-var `x14` completion flags drive transition for Link/Young Link hookshot and Samus grapple.
Respawn physics code can store vector-like state in `mv.co.common` fields with explicit float casts, so struct-field typing may lag behind semantics in matching code.
When matching fighter state transitions, preserving side-effect order around flags, motion changes, SFX, player-system notifications, and common cleanup calls is important.
naming_conventions: Existing project placeholder naming was retained for unknown functions and fields, e.g. `fn_800D54A4`, `ftCo_800D41C4`, `ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0`, `x2227_b6`, `x221F_b4`, `xDD4_itemVar`, and `mv.co.unk_deadup`.
Character-kind constants use `FTKIND_*`, with `FTKIND_LINK`, `FTKIND_CLINK`, `FTKIND_SAMUS`, and `FTKIND_PICHU` appearing in the matched code.

Fighter common functions in this area use `ftCo_` prefixes; Kirby-specific copied-ability/hat helpers use `ftKb_`; parts helpers use `ftParts_`; HSD object/matrix helpers use `HSD_JObj*` / `HSD_ObjAlloc`.
The PR did not introduce cleaned-up semantic names for many unknown fields; it prioritized matching and retained current decomp naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, and `PAD_STACK(12)` in matched functions to reproduce stack layout.
Used a dummy local byte array `u8 _[8];` in `ftCo_800D41C4` and `u8 sp14[0x88];` in `ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4` to control stack allocation.
Used `#pragma dont_inline` specifically for `ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4` to preserve asm layout.
Used nested scopes and duplicate `GET_FIGHTER`/temporary variables in `ftCo_800D41C4`, likely to match register allocation and live ranges.

Used explicit pointer casts such as `(FtPartsDesc*) new_var`, `(ftData_x8_x8*) &new_var->desc.vis_table`, `(u32*) ((u8*) new_var->hat_dynamics[4] + 4)`, and `*(u32*) &fp->x610_color_rgba[1]` for layout-sensitive data access.

Used float field reinterpretation writes like `*(f32*) &new_var->mv.co.common.x8` and `*(f32*) &new_var->mv.co.common.xC`, indicating struct typing was adapted for matching rather than fully corrected.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided slice.
One PR review is recorded in counts, but no review-comment excerpt or specific reviewer feedback was included.
The PR body states the work was split from #2345 and limited to fighter-related 100% matches; no additional rationale was provided in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find related work in PR #2345 to understand the broader pending-matches split and whether additional fighter stubs were deferred.
Search for later PRs renaming or documenting `ftKb_UnkMtxFunc0`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F14B4`, `ftCo_800D41C4`, and `fn_800D54A4`.
Inspect current headers/structs for `mv.co.common`, `mv.co.unk_deadup`, Kirby hat fields, and item-var hookshot/grapple fields to see if any casts from this PR were later eliminated.
Search for other `#pragma dont_inline` uses in fighter/Kirby code to reuse the same match tactic where inlining perturbs asm layout.
